name: Cookiecutter (Project Template Generator)

auth.notes: CLI tool; GitHub templates downloaded via HTTPS (no auth for public repos)

performance.notes: Template rendering is fast; network download from GitHub/URL adds latency

agent_readiness.notes: Project template generator using Jinja2. cookiecutter https://github.com/audreyfeldroy/cookiecutter-pypackage. cookiecutter gh:audreyfeldroy/cookiecutter-pypackage. Interactive prompts from cookiecutter.json. Python API: from cookiecutter.main import cookiecutter. output_dir = cookiecutter('path/to/template/', no_input=True, extra_context={'project_name': 'MyProject'}). Template vars: {{cookiecutter.project_name}}. Hooks: pre_gen_project.py / post_gen_project.py for setup scripts. Thousands of community templates on GitHub. Also see Copier for modern alternative.

gotchas: Templates from untrusted sources can execute arbitrary code via hooks
One-shot generation — doesn't help with updating existing projects from template changes
Jinja2 in filenames can cause path issues on some OS
Copier is a more modern alternative with update support
best_when: Generating new project structures from standardized templates
Team project standardization with consistent directory layouts
avoid_when: Updating existing projects when template changes (use Copier instead)
Templates from untrusted sources (hook security risk)
summary: Cookiecutter generates projects from Jinja2 templates. cookiecutter gh:org/template prompts for variables and creates project structure. Python API: cookiecutter('template/', extra_context={...}). Hooks (pre/post_gen_project.py) for setup. BSD-3. Thousands of community templates. One-shot generation only — doesn't sync with template updates. Copier is the modern alternative with update support. Best for team project standardization.
